title: eremophila macmillaniana
intro: Eremophila macmillaniana, also known as the Macmillan's emu bush, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia, characterized by its small, tubular flowers and shrubby growth habit. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of iridoid glycosides and phenolic compounds. These properties are said to offer health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and antimicrobial protection, making it a potential treatment for various skin conditions and infections. Eremophila macmillaniana can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, tinctures, or salves, which are typically made from the leaves, stems, or flowers of the plant.
